description:
You are a statistician building an interpretable regression model for residential property sale prices in Ames, Iowa. Training data (2051 observations, 80 features including SalePrice) is at /home/ga/RProjects/datasets/ames_train.csv. Test data (879 observations, same features without SalePrice) is at /home/ga/RProjects/datasets/ames_test.csv. Several features have substantial missing values where NA encodes absence rather than unknown data (e.g., NA in PoolQC means no pool, NA in GarageType means no garage).

Your final model must simultaneously satisfy four quality gates:
1. Accuracy: Root Mean Squared Logarithmic Error (RMSLE) below 0.16 on the held-out test set.
2. No multicollinearity: Variance Inflation Factor (VIF) below 5 for every predictor in the final model.
3. Goodness of fit: Adjusted R-squared above 0.82 on the training data.
4. No extreme influence: Maximum Cook's distance below 1.0.

Save all deliverables to /home/ga/RProjects/output/:
1. ames_predictions.csv with columns Id and predicted_price (879 rows, on the original dollar scale).
2. ames_diagnostics.csv with columns test_name, statistic, threshold, pass (one row per quality gate reporting your model's diagnostic values).
3. ames_coefficients.csv with columns term, estimate, std_error, p_value (all terms in your final model including the intercept).
4. ames_diagnostic_plots.png showing a 2x2 panel of standard regression diagnostics: residuals vs fitted, Normal Q-Q, Scale-Location, and Cook's distance.

Write your analysis in /home/ga/RProjects/ames_analysis.R. Pre-installed packages: car, lmtest, Metrics, ggplot2, dplyr. Login: username=ga, password=password123.

metadata.task_note: Requires iterative model building: fit regression, run diagnostics (VIF, Adj R-squared, Cook's D), remediate violations (drop collinear features, handle outliers, transform response), refit and recheck until all quality gates pass simultaneously.
